title: @noncancellable attribute on a block is syntactically inconsistent
description: ferrum-lang-concurrency.md §1.2.1 shows '@noncancellable { db.write(&data)? ... }' — the @attr syntax applied to a block, not an item. Ferrum's @ attributes are defined as item-level annotations (functions, structs, etc.). Using @attr on a block is a unique syntactic form not explained in the core spec. A caller seeing @noncancellable will not know how to parse it (is it a block attribute? a block decorator? an expression attribute?). Either: (a) block attributes are a general feature and the syntax spec must document them, or (b) @noncancellable should be a different syntactic form (a keyword, a macro call, or a scope modifier like 'scope critical { }').
